context: Colors become useful when you attach them to real things you already talk about - clothes, transport, and simple objects.
explanation: Start with three high-frequency colors: azul (blue), rojo (red), and verde (green). Then add common colors like amarillo, negro, and blanco. Use ser to describe color: mi camisa es azul.

examples.es: Azul es blue, rojo es red y verde es green.
examples.en: Azul is blue, rojo is red and verde is green

examples.note: Plural noun uses son + plural adjective.

examples.es: Ahora juega Word-Matching Test para emparejar colores.
examples.en: Now play Word-Matching Test to match color pairs
examples.note: Use Review -> Word-Matching Test after this lesson.

rejectIf: main color meaning is wrong or missing
object-color relation is reversed or broken

partialCreditExample.reason: core meaning is present but missing ser agreement pattern
